channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy navigates political turmoil amid military shake-up
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy faces mounting domestic political challenges as protests erupt in Kyiv over military leadership changes and corruption allegations. After replacing his army chi...

item.title: New Weekly HIV Treatment Pill Shows Promise in South Africa
item.description: A new once-weekly antiretroviral pill for HIV treatment has shown promising interim results in South Africa, according to studies presented at the AIDS 2026 conference. The pill, containing islatravir...

item.title: Tanzania government boosts youth training programs to enhance employment opportunities
item.description: The Tanzanian government has escalated its efforts to equip young people with essential skills through targeted training programs aimed at reducing unemployment and fostering economic growth. This ini...

item.title: UK's New Prime Minister Announces £2 Bus Fare Cap with Bus Aunty Bemi
item.description: Nigerian-born content creator Bus Aunty Bemi, known for her viral bus-side videos, collaborated with UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham to announce a £2 bus fare cap for England. The policy, set to take e...

item.title: Northrop Grumman's MRV-1 Satellite Extends Lifespan of Geostationary Satellites
item.description: On July 21, 2026, a Falcon 9 Block 5 rocket launched the MRV-1 satellite, developed by Northrop Grumman, into geostationary orbit. This satellite features two robotic arms designed for repairing and e...

item.title: Windsor Police and Partners Seize Over 1.7 Tonnes of Drugs in Major Cross-Border Operation
item.description: The Windsor Police Service, in collaboration with the Ontario Provincial Police (OPP), Canada Border Services Agency, U.S. Homeland Security Investigations, and the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administratio...

item.title: Tragic Drowning Incident at Essex Beach Claims Lives of Mother, Daughter, and Child
item.description: A tragic incident occurred on West Mersea beach in Essex when Shally Rahman (41) and her daughter Samiha Rahman (15) drowned while attempting to rescue a seven-year-old boy. The family, who had come f...

item.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal raises concerns over global nuclear arms race
item.description: The article discusses the implications of the US-Saudi nuclear deal, highlighting fears of a global nuclear arms race. President Trump's agreement with Saudi Arabia allows the kingdom to enrich nuclea...

item.title: Easywin Nigeria wraps up World Cup campaign with motorcycle giveaway
item.description: Easywin Nigeria has officially wrapped up its World Cup promotional campaign after the 2026 FIFA World Cup concluded with Spain lifting the trophy. The campaign, organized in collaboration with OPay, ...

item.title: Contagious Cancer in Fish Identified as Fourth Known Transmissible Cancer in Animals
item.description: Researchers in Vermont have identified a contagious form of cancer affecting brown bullhead catfish in Lake Memphremagog, marking the first known case of a transmissible cancer in fish and the fourth ...

item.title: Attorney General Todd Blanche Faces Legal Scrutiny Over Subpoena Authorization
item.description: Attorney General Todd Blanche's legal troubles deepened as a federal judge ruled that prosecutors failed to properly source information from the Justice Department's subpoenas targeting New York Times...

item.title: Seven Fishermen Arrested for Blast Fishing in Mindoro
item.description: Seven fishermen were arrested in Occidental Mindoro for allegedly using explosives to fish, according to Philippine Star reports. Authorities seized 900 kilos of dalagang bukid (yellow tail fusilier) ...

item.title: DOJ withdraws subpoenas against NYT reporters in Qatari jet investigation
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) has withdrawn subpoenas targeting New York Times (NYT) reporters involved in an investigation related to a Qatari jet story. The decision follows a court ruling th...

item.title: Gauteng Health Faces Criticism Over Delayed Surgeries at Boksburg Hospital
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has accused the Gauteng Department of Health of failing patients at Tambo Memorial Hospital in Boksburg due to delayed elective surgeries. The DA claims that the departmen...

item.title: Arusha Health Camp Provides Free Surgeries and Services to 258 Patients
item.description: During a medical camp in Arusha from July 13-21, 2026, over 28,871 citizens accessed health services. Among them, 258 patients with serious conditions like tumors, cancer, and cardiovascular diseases ...

item.title: Surviving Canberra's Harsh Winter: A Personal Account of Cold and Comfort
item.description: The article recounts the author's humorous and relatable struggles with Canberra's notoriously harsh winter climate. Despite technological advancements, Australians still rely on basic heating solutio...

item.title: CJP delegation to discuss government outreach initiatives in upcoming meeting
item.description: The article highlights the planned meeting between the CJP (Congress of Progressive Intellectuals) delegation and key government ministers scheduled for Friday. This gathering is part of the Centre's ...

item.title: Nollywood Actress Ngozi Nwosu Urges Support for Medical Treatments
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Ngozi Nwosu has made an emotional plea for support as she fights a serious health condition requiring urgent medical attention. The actress, who has been hospitalized for thr...

item.title: Pitso Mosimane Expresses Interest in Coaching Bafana Bafana Amid Squad Reforms
item.description: Pitso Mosimane, the experienced former coach of Mamelodi Sundowns and Al Ahly, has confirmed his interest in taking over as head coach of Bafana Bafana. The South African Football Association (Safa) h...

item.title: Delhi High Court establishes fast-track courts to address paper leak cases under new law
item.description: The Delhi High Court has established a dedicated fast-track court led by Judge Anu Grover Baliga to expedite the hearing of paper leak and exam cheating cases under the Public Examinations (Prevention...
